summary.intent: Fix formatting issues in auth_routes.rs that were causing CI to fail after a PR was pushed.
summary.outcome: Nightly rustfmt formatting was applied to expand inline if/else expressions in auth_routes.rs, committed, and pushed to unblock CI.

repo: The project uses nightly rustfmt (nightly-2025-11-30) which enforces expanded if/else block style rather than inline ternary-style if/else in certain contexts.
CI runs parallel local checks: fmt, biome, i18n, zizmor — all must pass for the pipeline to succeed.
The repo is at moltis-org/moltis and uses worktrees under ~/.superset/worktrees/moltis/.

code.finding: Inline `if secure { "; Secure" } else { "" }` must be expanded to multi-line block form to satisfy nightly rustfmt.
Same inline if/else pattern for secure_attr in a second function also required expansion.
Third occurrence of the secure_attr pattern plus a cookie format! macro that also needed reformatting.

workflow: Formatting failures are caught post-push by CI rather than pre-push, suggesting the pre-commit/pre-push hooks may not run nightly rustfmt locally.
The project uses `just` (Justfile) with a `format-check` recipe that wraps cargo fmt --check.

friction: Formatting failure was only discovered after the PR was already pushed, requiring an additional fix commit.
Nightly rustfmt's stricter formatting rules for inline if/else are not immediately obvious and can silently differ from stable rustfmt behavior.
